MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
NvidiaCodec C++

To run NvidiaCodec needs a host equipped with NVIDIA GPU. This example illuminates how to capture videos and hardcode the clips in H.264 with a NVIDIA GPU.
Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\Legacy_VC++\CmdTools\NvidiaCodec

Supported Hardware

Pro Capture Family and the host computer must be installed with a NVIDIA GPU

Features

  1. Capture and encode videos into H.264 using NVIDIA GPU.
  2. Save the encoded data as H.264 files.
  3. Select the capture channel from multiple devices by specifying the command value.

Workflow

  1. To get device version, initialize and enumerate all available devices, call: MWGetVersion, MWCaptureInitInstance, MWGetChannelCount
  2. To find the valid capture card according to the device info,, call: MWGetChannelInfoByIndex

  3. Conduct any of the following steps to open a channel:

    1. To open the specified channel according to the command parameter, call:MWOpenChannel;
    2. To open the first available channel by default when there is not a specified channel, call: MWGetDevicePath, MWOpenChannelByPath;
  4. To get the status of video signal, call: MWGetInputSpecificStatus
  5. To initialize and create an encoder, call: InitEncode, CreateEncode
  6. To ctreat a pool of buffers, and start video capture, call: MWPinVideoBuffer, MWStartVideoCapture
  7. To register for notifications, call: MWRegisterNotify
  8. To get one video frame triggered by the specified notifications, call: MWGetNotifyStatus, MWCaptureVideoFrameToVirtualAddress
  9. To save the encoded data as an H264 file, call: QueryEncodeEndStatus
  10. To unlock the memory, stop capture and free resources, call: MWUnregisterNotify, MWStopVideoCapture, MWUnpinVideoBuffer, MWCloseChannel, MWCaptureExitInstance

Result

NvidiaCodec
